title: Showing how a decision changed over time
question: When you open a decision, how should its past changes be shown so you can follow how it evolved?
rationale: Show the revision history as a vertical timeline inside the detail — the current state at the top, then each change below it newest-first with its date and why, down to when the decision was created. Opening a revision card from Recent jumps to and highlights that exact change. Chosen because a timeline reads as a story of how the call evolved, where the old flat list left you guessing what changed when; it stays inside the existing history fold, so the detail is still calm by default (d9).

options.label: A vertical timeline (now → each change → created)

tradeoffs.note: the evolution reads top-to-bottom as a story — current state, each change and why, back to creation
lives inside the existing collapsed history fold, so it doesn't add noise until you open it (d9)
clicking a revision card in Recent opens the timeline and highlights that exact entry (extends d38)
a new timeline renderer plus the highlight-on-open wiring; no change to how revisions are stored

options.label: A flat list of changes at the bottom (the old way)

tradeoffs.note: a plain list of 'was X — reason' lines doesn't show the order or the current state, so you can't see the arc of how it changed

options.label: A separate pop-up for each change

tradeoffs.note: focuses on one change, but you lose the whole-decision context around it
adds a second overlay type on top of the decision sheet — diverges from one-decision-at-a-time (d2, d9)
